originalTitle: Nigerian police arrest 15 after viral videos of alleged sexual assault at festival
title: Happy News from Nigeria: Police Help Everyone Stay Safe!
content:
Hello, Super Stars from Africa!

I am your friendly news reporter, here to share some important news from our big continent. We talk about how grown-ups are working hard to make sure everyone is safe and happy, especially girls and women.

What Happened in Nigeria?

Imagine a big, fun party called a festival! People often go to festivals to dance, sing, and have a good time. There was a festival in a country called Nigeria, which is a very big country in Africa.

Sometimes, even at fun parties, things can go wrong. Some grown-ups saw some videos on their phones. These videos showed people not being kind to others. It looked like some people were making other people, especially women, feel sad and unsafe.

Did you know? Nigeria is the country with the most people in all of Africa! It's a land full of different cultures and languages.

People Were Worried and Sad

When grown-ups saw these videos, many people felt very sad and worried. It's not nice when someone is treated badly, right? People started talking about it a lot, like a giant group chat for grown-ups. They used special words called a 'hashtag' to share their feelings.

The hashtag was like a big message that said: "Let's make sure everyone is treated kindly and safely." It was a way for people to say, "Hey, we need to protect our girls and women!"

Police Officers Are Helping!

Guess what? When bad things happen, we have special helpers called police officers! Police officers are like superheroes who wear uniforms. Their job is to keep everyone safe and make sure people follow the rules.

The police in Nigeria heard about what happened at the festival. They knew they needed to help. So, they started looking for the people who were not being kind in the videos.

Did you know? Police officers help in many ways! They find lost pets, help kids cross the road, and make sure people don't break rules that keep us safe.

Finding the People Who Were Unkind

The police worked very hard. They looked at the videos and talked to people. They wanted to understand everything. Their job is to make sure everyone is treated fairly, even the people who made mistakes.

Because the police are so good at their job, they found some people who were involved in being unkind. They asked about 15 people to come and talk to them. This is part of how they make sure rules are followed and everyone stays safe.

It's important to remember that when police talk to people, it means they are trying to figure out the truth. They want to make sure everyone learns how to be good neighbours and friends.

Why Is Kindness So Important?

Imagine playing with your friends. Everyone wants to feel happy and safe, right? No one likes it when someone pushes them or says mean things. It's the same for grown-ups!

Girls and women, just like boys and men, deserve to feel safe and happy everywhere they go – at home, at school, and even at big parties like festivals. Being kind means treating everyone with respect and making sure they feel comfortable.

Did you know? When you are kind to someone, it makes both of you feel good! It’s like a happy feeling that spreads around.

What Happens Next?

The police are still working on this. They will talk to everyone involved and make sure that justice is served. "Justice" means making sure things are fair and right for everyone.

It also means helping people learn that being unkind is not okay. We all need to learn to treat each other with love and respect. This helps build strong, happy communities.

How Can We All Help?

Even little super stars like you can help! Here are some ways:

  • Always be kind to your friends, family, and classmates.
  • If you see someone being unkind, tell a trusted grown-up (like a parent, teacher, or older sibling).
  • Remember that everyone deserves to feel safe and happy.
  • Use your kind words and helping hands to make the world a better place.

Did you know? Many African countries have special days to celebrate kindness and being good neighbours!

A Message of Hope

This story reminds us that even when bad things happen, there are always good people – like our police officers – who are working to make things right. It also shows that when many people speak up together, they can make a big difference.

Let's all promise to be kind and respectful to everyone, every day. That's how we build a super happy and super safe Africa for all of us!

Thank you for listening, super stars! See you next time for more happy news!
summary: Police in Nigeria are helping people stay safe after some videos showed unkind acts at a festival, reminding everyone to be kind and respectful.
whyMatters: This is important because everyone, especially girls and women, deserves to feel safe and happy, and grown-ups are working to make sure our communities are kind places for all.

originalTitle: Kenyan ex-foreign minister resurfaces after abduction fears
title: Kenyan ex-foreign minister resurfaces after abduction fears
content: The reported disappearance of Raphael Tuju had led to claims he may have been abducted.

originalTitle: Ex-teacher accused of Scotland school abuse found guilty of sex attack in South Africa
title: A Story of Speaking Up and Fairness in South Africa!
content:
Hello, amazing kids of Africa! It's your friendly news reporter here, ready to share an important story with you from sunny South Africa. Today, we're talking about something that happened in a place called Cape Town, a beautiful city right by the ocean. This story is about making things fair and safe for everyone, especially for children like you.

A Teacher and a Court

Imagine a school, just like yours. Most schools are happy places where children learn and play with kind teachers. But sometimes, very, very rarely, a grown-up might do something wrong. This story is about a grown-up named Mr. Iain Wares. He used to be a teacher a long, long time ago, even before your parents were born! He was found guilty of hurting a student in a wrong way.

What Does "Guilty" Mean?

When we say someone is "guilty," it means that a special group of people, called a court, listened very carefully to all the stories and found out that this person really did do something wrong. It means the rules were broken, and someone was hurt. The court in Cape Town decided that Mr. Wares did break some very important rules about keeping children safe.

A Brave Student Spoke Up

This happened a long time ago, in the 1980s. That's a super long time! But even after many, many years, the student who was hurt by Mr. Wares decided to be very brave and tell grown-ups what happened. This is super important! Sometimes, if something bad happens, it can feel scary to talk about it. But speaking up is one of the bravest things you can do. **Did you know?** There are special grown-ups, like police officers and social workers, whose job it is to help children feel safe and listen to them when they have something important to say.

What is a Court?

Let's talk about what a court is! A court is a very important place where grown-ups go to sort out problems and make sure everyone follows the rules, which we call laws. It's like a big meeting room, but with a super important person called a judge. The judge's job is to listen to everyone, think very hard, and then decide what is fair and true. They help make sure justice is done. In the court, there are also other grown-ups called lawyers. Lawyers help people tell their stories and explain things. It's like having a special helper to make sure your voice is heard clearly. The court in Cape Town listened to the story of the student and Mr. Wares for a very long time.

The Judge's Important Decision

After listening to everyone and looking at all the information, the judge in Cape Town made a big decision. The judge said that Mr. Wares was guilty. This means the court agreed that he did those wrong things to the student a long time ago. This decision helps to show that it's never okay to hurt someone, especially a child, and that rules are there to protect everyone. **Did you know?** Courts are not just in South Africa, but all over the world! They help people in many different countries solve problems fairly.

Why This Story is Important for Kids

This story might sound a little bit grown-up, but it has a super important message for you, amazing kids! It reminds us that: 1. **Your safety matters most.** Every child has the right to feel safe, loved, and happy. 2. **It's never your fault if a grown-up does something wrong.** If a grown-up ever makes you feel uncomfortable or does something that is not right, remember it is *never* your fault. 3. **Speaking up is powerful.** If someone hurts you or makes you feel unsafe, telling a trusted grown-up is the best thing to do.

Who Are Your Trusted Grown-ups?

It's good to know who your trusted grown-ups are. These are people you can always talk to, no matter what. They might be: * Your mom or dad * Your grandma or grandpa * A favourite auntie or uncle * A teacher you trust * Your school principal * A family friend * A police officer It's good to have at least three trusted grown-ups you know you can always talk to. If one person can't help, you can always try another! **Did you know?** In South Africa, there are special laws to protect children. These laws make sure that children are kept safe from harm and have the right to grow up happily.

Justice Means Making Things Right

When the court finds someone guilty, it's a way of bringing "justice." Justice means trying to make things right again and showing that bad actions have consequences. It helps the person who was hurt feel heard and helps other people learn that such things are not allowed. It also sends a clear message to everyone that children must be protected. This story from Cape Town shows that even after many years, justice can still happen. The brave student who spoke up is helping to make the world a safer place for everyone.

Feeling Safe at School and Everywhere

Most teachers and grown-ups are wonderful people who care deeply about children. They work hard to teach you new things, make you laugh, and keep you safe. This story is very rare. It's important to remember that most grown-ups are kind and trustworthy. But if you ever feel worried, or if someone tells you a secret you shouldn't keep, or if someone touches you in a way that feels wrong, remember to **TELL A TRUSTED GROWN-UP RIGHT AWAY!** It doesn't matter how small or big the worry is. Your feelings are important. This news from South Africa reminds us all to look out for each other and to be brave when we need to speak up. Always remember, you are special, and you deserve to be safe and happy every single day.
summary: A court in South Africa found a former teacher guilty of hurting a student a long time ago, showing that speaking up is brave and helps make things fair.
whyMatters: This story is important because it teaches us that it's brave to speak up if someone does something wrong, and that courts work to keep everyone, especially children, safe and treated fairly.

originalTitle: White gets first England call-up since 2022
title: Ben White is Back for England!
content:
Hello, Friends! Guess What?

Hello, my little superstars! I have some super exciting news from the world of football! Do you know what football is? It's that wonderful game where players kick a ball with their feet, trying to score a goal! It's super fun to play and watch. Today, we're talking about a very special football player named Ben White. He plays for a big team called Arsenal. And guess what? He has been asked to play for a super, super special team! It's the England team! That's a very big deal!

Who is Ben White?

Ben White is a super strong football player. He is like a superhero on the football field! He plays in a special spot called a "defender." What does a defender do? Well, think of it like this: if the other team is trying to score a goal, Ben is one of the brave players who tries to stop them! He protects his team's goal. He plays for a club team called Arsenal. Arsenal wears red and white shirts. They are a very famous team! Ben is very good at running fast and stopping the ball. He works very hard every day to be a fantastic player. * **Did you know?** The word "Arsenal" means a place where weapons are stored, but the football team is named after a factory that used to make weapons! No actual weapons in football, just balls!

What is the England Team?

Imagine your country, Africa! Now imagine the best football players from all over your country coming together to play as one big team. That's what the England team is! England is a country far away, across the ocean. They have a team with the very best players from England. When these players put on their special white shirts with three lions on them, they are playing for their whole country! It's a huge honour to wear that shirt. Ben White has played for England before, and now he is back! Everyone is very happy for him. * **Did you know?** The England football team's symbol has three lions! These lions are a very old symbol for England and show strength and courage! Roar!

What is a "Squad"?

Okay, so you know a football team has players, right? But for important games, they don't just pick 11 players. They pick a whole group of players! This big group is called a "squad." The squad has more players than can play at one time. Why? Because sometimes players get a little tired, or maybe they need a rest. So, the coach (that's the team's boss!) picks a squad. From this squad, he chooses who will play in each game. Ben White is now part of this special squad! This means he might get to play in the upcoming games.

What is a "Call-up"?

When we say Ben White got an "England call-up," it means he got a special message! It's like getting an invitation to a super important party. The coach of the England team, a very clever man named Gareth Southgate, sent Ben a message saying, "Ben, we need you! Come and play for our team!" This is a huge honour because it means the coach thinks Ben is one of the very best players in the country. It shows that all his hard work and training have paid off. Ben must be feeling very proud and excited! * **Did you know?** Football is the most popular sport in the world! Billions of people love to watch and play it! Maybe you do too!

Playing Against Other Countries

So, why did England pick Ben White now? Because they have some special games coming up! These games are called "friendlies." What are friendly games? They are not like super-duper important games that decide a big prize. They are games where teams practice and try out new players and new ways of playing. It's like a friendly match with your friends, but on a much bigger scale! England will play against two other countries. One country is called Uruguay (say: Yoo-roo-gwai). The other country is called Japan.

Who are Uruguay and Japan?

Uruguay is a country very far away, in South America. They have some amazing football players too! Japan is another country, also very far away, in Asia. They also have a very strong football team. So, these friendly games will be a good test for the England team. Ben White might get to play against these strong teams. Imagine flying across the world to play football! That's what some of these players do! It's an adventure! * **Did you know?** Uruguay was one of the first countries to win the biggest football prize in the world, the FIFA World Cup, way back in 1930!

Working Hard to Be the Best

Ben White's story teaches us something very important. To be good at anything, you need to work very, very hard! Ben trains almost every day. He runs, he kicks the ball, he learns new tricks, and he listens to his coaches. He eats healthy food and gets enough sleep. All these things help him to be a strong and healthy athlete. When you work hard, good things happen, just like Ben getting to play for his country again! * **Did you know?** Professional footballers often run many kilometres in just one game! Sometimes more than 10 kilometres! That's like running a very long race!

Teamwork Makes the Dream Work!

Football is not just about one player. It's about a whole team working together! Ben White plays as a defender, but he needs attackers to score goals, and midfielders to pass the ball. Everyone has a job, and everyone helps each other. When they work together, they are super strong. This is a great lesson for all of us. Whether it's playing a game, doing schoolwork, or helping at home, teamwork is super important. Ben White will be part of a team that wants to make their country proud.

What Will Happen Next?

Soon, Ben White will join up with the rest of the England squad. They will train together, eat together, and get ready for the friendly games against Uruguay and Japan. We will all be cheering for Ben and the England team! Maybe Ben will play super well and help England win! It's going to be very exciting to watch. We wish Ben White and the whole England team good luck! Maybe one day, you too will play for your country! Keep dreaming big, little champions! * **Did you know?** The first rules for football were written in England more than 150 years ago!
summary: Super player Ben White, who plays for Arsenal, has been asked to join the special England football team again to play friendly games against Uruguay and Japan.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that hard work can help you achieve big dreams, like Ben White playing for his country, and it's exciting for England to have a strong player back!
